name: Julian Priester
abstract: Julian Priester (born June 29, 1935) is an American jazz trombonist and occasional euphoniumist. He is sometimes credited as "Julian Priester Pepo Mtoto".

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: I would say maybe four or five at the most, that go back as deep as Marshall and Julian Priester and myself played in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I get with Eddie Henderson, start working with him and Julian Priester. We worked six months in a club.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah, I think that's it. Then there was a period -- Julian Priester -- for a minute it looks like and -- that was after -- right after you left, wasn't it? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes, that was in '70.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince DiMartino
about_quotes.evidence: when I played with Clark — with Lionel Hampton... the band was Jimmy Cleveland, Julian Priester... I was playing with Lionel Hampton.
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince DiMartino interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 8, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Turré
about_quotes.evidence: Some people like, you know, Lawrence Brown, or they might like J.J. or Curtis or Julian Priester or Frank Rossolino. I love them all.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Turré interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 29, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote a lyric to Julian Priester's 'Retribution.' ... Musicians were experimenting with me. Max, Julian, Booker, all of them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
